time remains practical. Compared to RA-SZZ, which requires 78 seconds per case for refactoring detection [ 36 ], AgenticSZZ is faster while achieving substantially higher accuracy. Controlled comparison confirms that improvements mainly stem from the TKG-guided paradigm, not merely from recent advances in the underlying LLM. Since AgenticSZZ uses DeepSeek-V3.2 while LLM4SZZ employs GPT-4o-mini, one might question whether AgenticSZZ’s improvements are due to architectural contributions or simply a more capable LLM. To isolate the architectural impact, we conducted a controlled comparison (Table 4 ) where both approaches use DeepSeek-V3.2. LLM4SZZ with DeepSeek achieves comparable F1-scores to the original GPT-4o- mini version (e.g., 0.590 vs 0.588 on DS_LINUX), indicating that DeepSeek is at least as capable for this task. Comparing against this controlled baseline, AgenticSZZ achieves a 14.0% relative improvement in overall F1-score (0.642 vs 0.563), due to gains of 11.2% on DS_LINUX and 36.0% on DS_GITHUB. On DS_APACHE, both approaches achieve comparable F1-scores (0.480 vs 0.467), consistent with the blame complexity challenges discussed above. The best-vs-best comparison is statistically significant (McNemar’s test, 𝑝 < 0 . 001, Cohen’s 𝑔 = 0 . 27, large effect); even our worst run outperforms their best ( 𝑝 < 0 . 001, 𝑔 = 0 . 25), demonstrating that the TKG-guided search paradigm, not merely LLM capability or run variance, drives AgenticSZZ’s effectiveness.
• AgenticSZZ outperforms all baselines with F1-score improvements up to 26.6%; even its worst run beats the best baseline ( 𝑝 < 0 . 001).
• Controlled comparison confirms improvements stem from the TKG-guided paradigm, not LLM selection. Implication: Reframing BIC identification as graph search over a TKG advances the state-of-the-art.

6.2 RQ2: What is the Contribution of Each AgenticSZZ Component? 6.2.1 Motivation. AgenticSZZ integrates two core components: a Temporal Knowledge Graph (TKG) for search space construction and expansion, and an LLM agent for intelligent navigation. Understanding each component’s contribution is essential for validating our design decisions and guiding future research. We conduct an ablation study to answer: Is the TKG necessary, or can an LLM agent reason effectively over raw blame results alone? Does the fallback strategy for
